data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a Linux-only CI job that runs the test suite with `BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail'` exported, surfacing SIGPIPE-class failures that macOS local development cannot reproduce.
**Why**: Closes G12-M3 from the 2026-04-26 retrospective. Pattern 4 (Linux-strict, macOS-lenient pipefail behavior) has produced silent regressions caught only at release time. A Linux-strict CI leg makes the divergence visible at PR time.
**Scope**:
- IN: New CI job in the staged needs:-chain (positioned as a sibling of the standard tests job, on Linux only) that exports `BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail'` before invoking the test runner. Job emits a stable check-context name for inclusion in the Ruleset's required-check list (coordinated with `fc8b-aab0`).
- OUT: Modifying tests to remove or rewrite pipefail-incompatible patterns — this story surfaces failures, fix-bug or new bug tickets handle the resolutions. Adding the job to the Ruleset's required-check list — that is part of `fc8b-aab0`. macOS-equivalent strictness — Pattern 4 is Linux-specific by design.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, every PR triggers a Linux-only CI job (e.g., `tests-pipefail-strict`) that exports `BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail'` and runs the test suite, and the job's check context appears as a status check on every PR
  ← Satisfies: "A Linux-only test-job variant runs the test suite with BASH_OPTS='-eo pipefail' exported, is included in the Ruleset's required-check list, and surfaces SIGPIPE-class failures that macOS local development cannot reproduce"
- When this story is complete, a PR that introduces a SIGPIPE-class regression (e.g., a piped command whose head process exits early) causes the pipefail-strict job to fail while the standard tests job passes — demonstrating the divergence detection works
  ← Satisfies: "surfaces SIGPIPE-class failures that macOS local development cannot reproduce"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic (workflow file structural test verifying the job exists, runs on Linux, and exports BASH_OPTS)
  ← Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Existing test suite may have pipefail-incompatible patterns that fail immediately when this job lands. Sequence: ship the job behind `continue-on-error: true` first, observe failures, file fix-bug tickets, then flip to required (decision deferred to `/dso:implementation-plan`).
- [Performance] A second full test run roughly doubles CI cost per PR. Consider whether to run only the bash-script test subset (skip pytest) since SIGPIPE divergences are bash-specific. Decision deferred to `/dso:implementation-plan`.
- [Testing] The demonstration test (a synthetic SIGPIPE regression) belongs in tests/ as a permanent canary so the divergence-detection capability is exercised, not just claimed.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a project maintainer, I can rely on a Linux-strict pipefail CI job to surface SIGPIPE failures invisible on macOS
